settings.title: We Now Install Native Wildflower Meadows
settings.excerpt: Our new meadow service swaps thirsty lawn for a low-water mix that feeds local bees from May to October.
settings.body:
Lawns are lovely until July, when they need mowing every week and watering every other day. This year we added a service we have wanted to offer for a long time: native wildflower meadows.

We strip the turf, sow a regional seed mix of yarrow, oxeye daisy, and red clover, and walk you through the first season so you know what to expect. The first year looks sparse. The second year it sings.

A meadow needs cutting once or twice a year instead of weekly. It also brings in bees, hoverflies, and butterflies that a clipped lawn never will.

Ask us for a meadow quote when we visit, and we will tell you honestly whether your site suits it.
